📌 [...] 📌

Hey Product Hunt! 👋 We're back, \\CodeNearby\\ is relaunching with a fresh UI and a bunch of new features. If you tried us before, this isn't the same app. If you haven't, now's the time. You won't be disappointed. \\What's new & what it does:\\ - 🎨 Rebuilt UI, cleaner, faster, way less clunky - 🔍 Search devs by skill, interest, location - 🤖 AI-Connect, describe who you need in plain English, get matched via GitHub - 💬 Real-time chat to actually talk to them - 📢 Developer feed for sharing updates/snippets - 🎭 Virtual gatherings, polls, events, discussions - 🐙 GitHub-synced profiles, zero manual setup It's fully \\open-source\\, self-host it, contribute, or just poke around: github.com/subh05sus/codenearby Go have a look and find your dev partner. Feedback, bugs, roasts, all welcome. 🙏

# CodeNearby 2.0 Review

Last modified: |  Fred Magni

Tired of navigating the digital wilderness for your next coding adventure? CodeNearby 2.0 is here to be your ultimate dev matchmaker. Billed as “Tinder for developers,” this platform helps you discover coding partners, co-founders, or mentors nearby, transforming solo sprints into collaborative triumphs. No more endless scrolling on generic platforms; CodeNearby makes connections happen where it matters most – by skills, interests, and location.

CodeNearby 2.0 Review

Uniqueness 71%

The uniqueness score is 71%.

Utility 75%

The utility score is 75%.

Innovation 72%

The innovation score is 72%.

Ease of Use 86%

The ease of use score is 86%. [...] ## Your Tribe is Waiting: Why Developers Choose CodeNearby

CodeNearby is built for how developers actually work, offering a superior networking experience compared to traditional platforms. It’s perfect for anyone seeking to expand their professional circle, debug complex issues, or kickstart a side project.

Whether you’re an indie hacker, an SDE at a fintech, or fresh out of a research background, CodeNearby connects you with like-minded individuals who understand your stack and your struggles. Find a design partner, a mentor, or simply someone to bounce ideas off – your next pivotal connection is just a click away. [...] Ease of Use 86%

The ease of use score is 86%.

Visit Website  Have an AI Tool? Submit it here!

## Unleash Your Dev Superpowers with CodeNearby

CodeNearby 2.0 is packed with features designed to streamline your developer networking experience, making it easier than ever to find your people and build something awesome together.

results.title: CodeNearby
results.content:
Platform

## This is the start of something new

Finding the right developer to work with should be easy. Whether you want a coding partner, to share ideas, or join a project, CodeNearby makes it simple.

I believe

"Connections should be easy to make because great ideas grow faster together..."

Latest Release: v2.0.0

## Additions

 Redesigned UI with refreshed look and improved visual consistency across all pages
 Integrated transactional email system — welcome emails, friend request notifications, comment alerts, and gathering updates
 Added post milestone emails when your post hits 10, 50, or 100 upvotes
 Re-engagement emails to keep you connected with the developer community

View Changelog history

ReactTypeScriptRustPythonGoSolidityNext.jsKubernetesMLWebAssembly [...] Pair coding

Code review

Hackathon

Study group

### Direct Messaging

Chat one-on-one. Share GitHub links, code snippets, and ideas in real time.

How it works

## Three steps. Zero friction.

1

### Sign up with GitHub

One click with your GitHub account. Your profile, repos, and activity come with you — no forms to fill, no setup required.

2

### Discover & Connect

Browse developers by location and skill, or let AI-Connect find the right people through natural conversation. Send a request, start a conversation.

3

### Build Together

Join gatherings, co-work on projects, discuss ideas in the feed. From first connection to shipped product — CodeNearby keeps you close.

Built in public. Open source. Forever free.

CodeNearby is MIT-licensed and community-driven.

Star on GitHub [...] ## Everything you need to connect

### Discover Developers

Find developers nearby by location, skills, and interests. Your next coding partner, co-founder, or mentor is closer than you think.

ReactPythonTypeScriptRustGoSolidity

### AI-Connect

Natural conversations to find GitHub developers by skill, location, or vibe.

### GitHub Integration

Sync your profile, showcase projects, find devs with matching coding interests.

### Discussions

Debug together, share ideas, and grow with your local developer community.

### Global Community

Connect globally, collaborate locally. Build your network without borders.

### Virtual Gatherings

Join online meetups, pair programming sessions, and code reviews with developers in your timezone.

results.title: Why isn't there Tinder for coding partners? - DEV Community
results.content:
michaeltharrington profile image
Subscriber

Awesome post, James!

I really think you're onto something here...

I've had a similar idea about an app for matching up folks to play music together. My idea kinda hinged on pairing up people that are in the same area geographically, which I believe must be how Tinder works granted I don't truly know (I've been with the same person for 15 years so luckily missed out on online dating, haha).

With finding a coding partner, I don't think you'd need to pair people up that are physically nearby each other, so that's kind of cool that you wouldn't have to worry about that. Still, it'd be cool to give folks the option to filter to people that are near them in case they want to meet up and work on a project in person together. [...] gmf002 profile image

Remote pairing is tolerable. But, there's nothing quiet like passing the keyboard back and forth in person. An ex-coworker had the IT department install two keyboards, mice, and an extra monitor that he mirrored. When we'd pair, one person sat at each keyboard/monitor and we'd just take turns writing code. He was big on TDD, so it was usually one person wrote a test, the other wrote the code, then another test and repeat. It flowed really well. I've yet to be on a remote pairing session that flowed as well as that.

So, maybe you're on to something here. :)

robertgid profile image

It isn't quite the same, but things like VS Code Live Share can partially emulate that experience, letting you follow along and write code simultaneously. [...] sasirekhamurakonda profile image

Enjoyed reading the article. Good idea actually.

danielkv profile image

Well, despite many communities around, I've never seen something that specific. I do think might be a good ideia! I'm really interested! If you have any interest on building a mvp, reach out. I'll leave my contact info in my profile.

uzair004 profile image

Ben Awad (youtuber) created a VSCode extension kindda same product you are talking about, later sold it within weeks.

james_palermo_bc208e463e4 profile image

Never heard of it.

khokon profile image

Award 😂😂😂😂

uzair004 profile image

I mean Awad :D

berviantoleo profile image

This is a great idea. 🙂
